completed_work: Mapped current provider L0 invariant ValueError messages into structured code, message, path, and optional chunk_id entries.
Updated ask_provider.py to emit structured validation.l0_invariants results.
Updated compare_providers.py to emit structured validation.l0_invariants results.
Added tests for structured success, structured duplicate-cid failure, list-shape error coding, and CLI JSON output.
key_decisions: Keep structured validation output additive and limited to the existing --validate-l0-invariants JSON surface.
Do not introduce new validation semantics while adding structured error fields.
Version the validation result as v1 and expose machine-readable code/path fields alongside the existing message.
next_actions: Consider collecting all validation failures, adding L0 chunk JSON validation using the actual L0ChunkSummary.to_dict shape, malformed L0 store diagnostics, or guarded explicit L0 context-pack inclusion.
risks: Structured error mapping is message-derived, so future ValueError message changes can affect code/path mapping.
Only the first validation failure is reported because validate_provider_l0_invariants still raises on first violation.
The structured codes cover current summary-layer checks only, not L0 chunk-file schema or citation/span validation.
summary_text: AI-Lab added structured provider L0 invariant validation result output. Existing summary-layer validator behavior remains unchanged, but provider_l0_invariant_validation_result now wraps validation status as version v1 with ok/errors and structured error entries containing code, message, path, and optional chunk_id. ask_provider.py and compare_providers.py use this structured result for --validate-l0-invariants JSON output. This does not add new validation rules, does not validate citation/span formats, does not validate L0 chunk files, and does not change prompt assembly, context packs, L0 creation/loading, or provider calls. Full suite passed with 245 tests.
